note: Seven-day LTV snapshot. Segment-level signal — applies equally across all ages within the segment.

poor.label: iOS Poor
poor.insight: No clear winner. B's higher CVR (+0.67pp) is not statistically significant (p=0.20). ARPU is virtually identical. A has a borderline LTV edge (p=0.091) driven by genuine renewals — no reactivation noise. B's higher renewal rate (1.01 vs 0.85) may close that gap over a longer window.
poor.recommendation_impact: Extend the test for more data, or default to B on the basis of slightly higher CVR and renewal rate. Do not read A's LTV edge as conclusive.

rich.label: iOS Rich
rich.insight: B wins on CVR (p=0.035), ARPU, and total revenue. A's LTV-per-converter lead ($42.90 vs $35.48) is entirely explained by 55.7% reactivations in Group A vs 1.4% in B — reactivated users inflate LTV without reflecting new pricing performance. Removing reactivations: A $43.81 vs B $35.61, but B's volume advantage overrides this.
rich.recommendation_impact: Strong confirmation for B. The reactivation anomaly artificially inflates A's LTV — it is not a quality signal. B leads at every renewal horizon (F0 through F4) on cumulative ARPU.

android_poor.label: Android Poor
android_poor.insight: B wins on CVR (+1.13pp), ARPU, and revenue — but A has the most genuine per-converter quality edge of any segment (LTV $23.00 vs $16.57, p=0.073) with zero reactivation noise. However, n=22 converters in A is too small to be conclusive. The ARPU gap is narrowing over time ($0.121 at F0 → $0.052 at F4), suggesting convergence.
android_poor.recommendation_impact: B is the right rollout call on volume and revenue, but monitor LTV post-rollout. Note: Android Poor has a 15–18% pricing mismatch rate vs 1–2% for iOS — fix consistency before reading LTV at face value.

android_rich.label: Android Rich
android_rich.insight: The cleanest result: zero reactivations in either group, so B's advantage is fully unconfounded. B leads on CVR (+3.00pp, p=0.059), ARPU (+$0.317), and total revenue (+$209, +24.5%). A's LTV-per-converter edge (p=0.078) is based on only 22 converters and does not override B's volume and ARPU dominance. B leads at every cumulative ARPU horizon.
android_rich.recommendation_impact: Strongest LTV confirmation for B across all segments. No confounding factors. Switzerland is the only country where A leads ARPU ($4.22 vs $2.61) — worth monitoring at the country level post-rollout.
